what_it_does: Defines frozen Pydantic output schemas, a mandatory neutral fragment, and verbatim canonical prompts for the dragnet coarse/fine/design extraction role family, plus maps that bind role ids to those schemas and prompts.
observations: Three Pydantic BaseModel classes (Coarse, Fine, Design) define expected JSON output shapes.
Coarse and Fine prompts both instruct neutral description of content; Design prompt asks for design binding.
NEUTRAL_FRAGMENT is a constant sentence that dragnet_coarse prompts must carry verbatim.
COARSE_PROMPT, FINE_PROMPT, and DESIGN_PROMPT are full canonical prompt strings intended for exact-match enforcement.
DRAGNET_FAMILY maps role ids to schema classes; DRAGNET_PROMPTS maps role ids to canonical prompt strings.
File lives in contracts/ and claims to be a zero-dependency spine so it is non-authorable.
Design fields (resolves_into, resolution) are described as additive visual-DNA fields that are null/absent for non-DNA spaces.
standouts: Exact-match canonical prompt enforcement is used as a freeze mechanism to prevent prompt negation bypasses.
The neutral fragment is treated as an irreducible guard against non-neutral authored wording.
Role-to-schema and role-to-prompt maps centralize the frozen definitions for a runtime safety door.
conventions: Heavy inline comments explain governance intent (D1, D3, D4, OPEN-1, P5-hardened).
Uses Pydantic BaseModel for schema definitions.
Claims immutability by construction rather than by convention.
concerns: The file claims a single stored superset record (D1/D2 near-lock), but no combined superset schema class is defined; Coarse, Fine, and Design are independent classes.
Coarse is described as a subset of Fine, yet Fine does not contain Coarse fields (about, kind, touches).
No runtime enforcement code is present in this file; it only supplies definitions to be checked elsewhere.
The exact-match prompt freeze is brittle if legitimate prompt fixes are needed, requiring edits to this spine file.
notes: Frozen schema and verbatim prompt contract for the dragnet extraction role family, intended to be imported and verified by a runtime safety door.
questions: Where is the actual stored-record superset class that combines Coarse, Fine, and Design fields?
How does the safety door perform the exact prompt match and schema identity check?
Where are the dragnet role registry rows authored and stored?
What do D1, D3, D4, OPEN-1, and P5 refer to in this codebase?
How is the neutral fragment verified if a row adds wording around it?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to freeze one dragnet extraction superset plus canonical prompts; actual file supplies separate schema classes and prompt strings but lacks the unified record schema and enforcement logic, so the 'one superset' claim is not directly realized here.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make dragnet schemas and prompts unchangeable at the role-registry level by anchoring them in a non-authorable contracts file that a safety door can compare by exact import-path identity.
novelty: Using verbatim canonical prompt strings and an exact-match requirement as a structural guard against prompt-injection-style negation of neutrality instructions.
contribution: Provides authoritative Pydantic schema classes and canonical prompt templates that a dragnet role registry and safety door can reference by import path, establishing a single point of definition for extraction output shapes and prompt wording.
summary_for_embedding: Frozen Pydantic schemas and verbatim canonical prompts for the dragnet coarse, fine, and design extraction role family, with role-to-schema and role-to-prompt maps for runtime verification.
intention_for_embedding: To make dragnet output schemas and prompts non-authorable by defining them in a zero-dependency contracts file that a runtime safety door can verify by exact import-path identity.

symbols.does: Defines the coarse dragnet extraction output shape: about, kind, and touches.
symbols.description: A Pydantic BaseModel class representing the coarse-grained neutral extraction result, capturing a one-phrase description, a content kind, and topic tags.

symbols.does: Defines the fine dragnet extraction output shape: summary, entities, claims, relations, and open_questions.
symbols.description: A Pydantic BaseModel class representing a deeper neutral extraction, listing named entities, stated claims/relations, and unresolved questions.

symbols.does: Defines the design dragnet extraction output shape: resolves_into and resolution.
symbols.description: A Pydantic BaseModel class representing the visual/design binding extraction, mapping content to design elements and dimension-keyed context points such as line, opacity, colour_role, and shape.
